title: Conjecture 1.1 (Las Vergnas–Meyniel Conjecture A)
statement_text: For every $t$, all the $t$-colourings of a graph with no $K_{t}$-minor form a single equivalence class.
context_text: Las Vergnas and Meyniel introduced this in 1981 as a reconfiguration counterpoint to Hadwiger's conjecture, after proving the analogous result for $K_5$-minor-free graphs. It neither implies Hadwiger's conjecture nor is implied by it.
paper_contribution: Disproves this conjecture by constructing, for every $\varepsilon>0$ and all large enough $t$, a graph with no $K_t$-minor whose $(\frac{3}{2}-\varepsilon)t$-colourings are not all Kempe equivalent (Theorem 1.3).
paper_title: On a recolouring version of Hadwiger's conjecture

title: Conjecture 1.2 (Las Vergnas–Meyniel Conjecture A')
statement_text: For every $t$ and every graph with no $K_{t}$-minor, every equivalence class of $t$-colourings contains some $(t-1)$-colouring.
context_text: Proposed by Las Vergnas and Meyniel in 1981 as a related but weaker statement than Conjecture 1.1, assuming Hadwiger's conjecture holds.
paper_contribution: Disproves this conjecture as a consequence of Theorem 1.3, which produces a frozen colouring with no path to a $(t-1)$-colouring in its Kempe equivalence class.
paper_title: On a recolouring version of Hadwiger's conjecture

title: Conjecture 1.4 (Las Vergnas–Meyniel Conjecture C)
statement_text: For any $t$, any graph that admits a quasi-$K_{t}$-minor admits a $K_{t}$-minor.
context_text: Las Vergnas and Meyniel introduced the notion of quasi-$K_p$-minor (where the bags $B_i$ need not be connected but each $B_i\cup B_j$ must induce a connected subgraph) and conjectured it is equivalent to the standard minor. The conjecture had been verified for $t\leqslant 10$.
paper_contribution: Strongly disproves this conjecture for large $t$ via Theorem 1.5, which constructs graphs admitting a quasi-$K_t$-minor but no $K_{ct}$-minor for some explicit $c<1$; the precise infimum $c$ is left as Question 1.6.
paper_title: On a recolouring version of Hadwiger's conjecture

title: Question 1.6
statement_text: What is the infimum $c$ such that for any large enough $t$, there is a graph that admits a quasi-$K_{t}$-minor but no $K_{ct}$-minor?
context_text: Follows from the strong disproof of Conjecture 1.4. The paper establishes the bounds $\frac{1}{2}\leqslant c\leqslant\frac{2}{3}$; the trivial upper bound $c\leqslant 1$ comes from the fact that every quasi-$K_{2t}$-minor-free graph admits a $K_t$-minor.
paper_title: On a recolouring version of Hadwiger's conjecture

title: Question 1.7
statement_text: Is there a constant $c^{\prime}$ such that for every $t$, all the $c^{\prime}\cdot t$-colourings of a graph with no $K_{t}$-minor form a single equivalence class?
context_text: Theorem 1.3 shows $c'\geqslant\frac{3}{2}$ if such a constant exists. The best known upper bound comes from results of Kostochka and Thomason showing degeneracy $O(t\sqrt{\log t})$ for $K_t$-minor-free graphs, combined with the fact that all $k$-colourings of $d$-degenerate graphs are Kempe equivalent for $k>d$.
paper_title: On a recolouring version of Hadwiger's conjecture
